title: Mobile database encryption keys should not be disclosed
description: Disclosing encryption keys used for mobile databases can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data. Applications must ensure that such keys are never hard-coded, logged, or otherwise exposed in the codebase or at runtime.
recommendation: Store encryption keys securely using platform-provided secure storage mechanisms (e.g., Android Keystore, iOS Keychain) and never expose them in code or logs.
impact: If encryption keys are disclosed, attackers can decrypt sensitive data stored in mobile databases, leading to data breaches and loss of user trust.

checks.message: Store encryption keys securely using platform-provided secure storage mechanisms (e.g., Android Keystore, iOS Keychain) and never expose them in code or logs.
